summary: Phase 2 backend testing for The Girl House. 27/27 pytest tests passed (100%). Verified: real Object Storage upload (PNG OK, non-image rejected, >6MB rejected, unauthorized rejected), public file serving with correct Content-Type, change-password (short/wrong/success + revert flow), reviews CRUD with approval flow auto-recalculating product rating & reviews_count, testimonials public-vs-admin filter (5 seeded) + create/update/delete, Paymob/Fawry/Stripe all return 501, and existing flows (login, products, settings, stats, coupons) still work. Admin password verified back to Admin@123 after change-password test.

minor.issue: [Carry-over from iter1] Brute-force lockout still relies on x-forwarded-for; not re-tested in Phase 2. Already documented and acknowledged.

Uses TestimonialCreate (full body) so partial updates (e.g., toggling is_active only) require resending the full object. Acceptable but consider a TestimonialUpdate model with optional fields for parity with products/settings.
Returns 200 with {'deleted':0} on missing id instead of 404 (consistent with categories/coupons; cosmetic).
If the file exists in object storage but no db.files record, response still 200 (record is queried with find_one but only used to override media_type). Not a bug, just note.

action_items: (carry-over) Fix brute-force lockout to use X-Forwarded-For first IP or rate-limit by email only.
Optional: switch DELETE handlers (testimonials/reviews/categories/coupons/products) to return 404 when nothing was deleted, for API consistency.
Optional: add TestimonialUpdate (all-optional) for partial PATCH-style updates.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1460 lines — split into routers (auth, products, orders, reviews, testimonials, files) when convenient.
global storage_key in init_storage() is fine for single-process uvicorn; under multi-worker would need a shared cache. Currently OK with re-init on 403.

approve_review correctly recalculates rating/reviews_count; delete_review does NOT recalc — so deleting an approved review leaves stale aggregates on the product. Suggest mirroring the recalc logic in delete_review when the deleted review was is_approved=True.
Upload reads the entire file into memory before size check (await file.read()) — for 6MB cap this is fine, but if cap grows consider streaming and aborting early.
Public review submission has no rate limiting / spam protection — spam risk on a public endpoint.

/api/files/{path:path} relies on Emergent storage round-trip for every request and has Cache-Control 7d; consider redirecting to a signed/CDN URL if the storage service supports it to reduce backend bandwidth.
test_credentials: admin@thegirlhouse.eg / Admin@123 - verified working AFTER change-password revert flow
seed_data_creation: Tests created and cleaned up: 1 review (TEST_Reviewer) — approved then deleted; 1 testimonial (TEST_Inactive→TEST_Updated) — created/updated/deleted. Admin password temporarily changed and reverted. No residual TEST_ data from this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase 2 endpoints pass. Use /app/backend/tests/test_phase2.py for regression. Admin creds are Admin@123. The minor issue about delete_review not recalculating product rating is the main correctness gap worth fixing if Phase 3 needs it. backend_test.py from iter1 still has the brute-force test as the only pre-existing failure.
rca of the issue: n/a — no blocking issues this iteration.
